overview.description: The JPMorgan Active High Yield ETF (JPHY) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in high yield fixed income. The fund actively manages a portfolio of high yield bonds issued by US and foreign corporations. The fund exclusively selects USD-denominated securities while maintaining a weighted average portfolio maturity of three to ten years. JPHY was launched on Jun 24, 2025 and is issued by JPMorgan Chase.

data.title: J.P. Morgan to Transfer 14 ETFs From Current Exchanges
data.text: NEW YORK, March 27, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- J.P. Morgan Asset Management today announced the upcoming exchange listing transfer of 14 ETFs from their current exchanges including the NASDAQ Stock Market L...

data.title: Bond-ETF inflows surge as demand returns for Treasurys
data.text: Demand for fixed-income exchange-traded funds has surged in the past week, as U.S. government debt reversed outflows.

data.title: JPMorgan Launches $2 Billion ETF as Exec Backs High-Yield Debt Over Private Credit
data.text: Finite opportunities in private credit are creating public, high-yield debt opportunities, J.P. Morgan Asset Management CEO George Gatch said, according to a Wednesday (June 25) Bloomberg report.

data.title: J.P. Morgan Asset Management launches its largest active ETF
data.text: J.P. Morgan Asset Management on Wednesday announced the launch of its largest active exchange-traded fund anchored by a $2 billion investment from an external institutional client.
